conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?

Exhausted. I've been working a lot of overtime to get everything done on time, and my social life is basically non-existent. I just want to sit on the couch and watch Netflix without thinking about spreadsheets for five minutes.
You mentioned being exhausted—how has that been affecting your sleep?

I'm getting maybe four or five hours a night. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there, thinking about everything I need to do tomorrow. It's like my brain is still on the clock even when I'm asleep.
You mentioned just wanting to watch Netflix—do you find you're still able to enjoy the shows you watch?
Honestly? No. I'm just watching the same shows I always watch because it’s easy. I'm not really taking it in. I’m just staring at the TV trying to relax.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about everything you need to do, what kind of thoughts go through your head?
Just the usual stuff. ‘I should have done this differently’ or ‘I should have spoken up earlier’. Just beating myself up over things I can’t change. It’s pointless, but it keeps me up.
You mentioned beating yourself up over things you can't change—how does that affect how you see yourself?
It makes me feel like a failure. I know I'm not failing in the classical sense, but I feel like I’m failing myself. Like I’m not living up to my own standards.
When you think about the future, does it feel like you'll be able to get back on track?
I don't know. I used to think I was a reliable person, but now I feel like I'm just barely holding it together. If I keep going like this, I don't know what I'll look like in a year.
You mentioned feeling like you're just barely holding it together—does that feeling ever make you feel on edge or irritable with people?
All the time. I snap at my friends over stupid things. I just feel like I’m running on empty, and nobody else gets it.
When you say you feel like you're running on empty, does that make it hard to even think about doing things you used to enjoy, like seeing friends?
Yeah. I look at the calendar and see a social event coming up, and I just feel like I can’t do it. I don't have the energy to put on a mask and pretend to be someone I'm not.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
I don't know. Maybe it started when I realized I wasn't getting any younger. I used to think I'd get to this point and figure it out, but now I just feel like I’m just going through the motions.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. Work, relationships, hobbies... it’s all just noise. I used to find meaning in those things, but now they just feel like things I have to do.
